public_ablation.worldview: Direct JEPA latent can be decoded straight into target probabilities.
public_ablation.interpretation: A latent can look coherent locally and still be a toxic action head.
public_ablation.paper_use: Use as the negative reason HS-JEPA needs listener/action-health/route modules.

public_ablation.worldview: Masked-family S2 JEPA translator is already an action-safe decoder.
public_ablation.interpretation: S2 matters, but S2-only translation without row-target assignment is not safe.
public_ablation.paper_use: Separates target listener discovery from action decoding.

public_ablation.worldview: Human-social latent can be directly materialized as a tail correction.
public_ablation.interpretation: Human/social context remains useful as latent context, not as direct public-safe movement.

public_ablation.worldview: S1/S4 objective-stage action is a safe objective sleep correction route.
public_ablation.interpretation: Objective-stage movement is not automatically safe; route and listener constraints matter.
public_ablation.paper_use: Explains why route-conserving decoder is needed.

public_ablation.interpretation: The big jump came from finding hidden row-target public-state support, not from larger model capacity.

public_ablation.worldview: Q2 support rows expose a broader hidden human-state vector.
public_ablation.interpretation: Freezing Q2 support and translating the rest of the row vector improved the public sensor.
public_ablation.paper_use: Supports row-target assignment as hidden-state route recovery.

public_ablation.worldview: Adding extra subjective target route cells should improve the Q2 route.
public_ablation.interpretation: Target route was plausible, but row placement/support did not add public value.
public_ablation.paper_use: Assignment quality dominates plausible target semantics.

public_ablation.worldview: Dual-state Pareto gate is a private/action-grade decoder.
public_ablation.interpretation: The gate remains useful as toxicity stress, not as a broad action head.
public_ablation.paper_use: Supports action-health diagnostic rather than direct correction.

public_ablation.worldview: Q3 repair or target-XOR split is the decisive missing axis.
public_ablation.interpretation: Both tied at public precision; the common body matters more than the branch distinction.
public_ablation.paper_use: Avoid overclaiming target-specific repairs as core architecture.

stress_ablation.worldview: Route-conserving bridge selection is just lucky cell picking.
stress_ablation.interpretation: Selected bridge bundles lower route energy far more than random feasible alternatives.

stress_ablation.worldview: S2 is not special inside the objective-stage bridge decoder.
stress_ablation.sensor: S2 usage random feasible bundle null
stress_ablation.interpretation: S2 appears as listener/hub in all selected interpretable bundles, far above null.

one_sentence: The public sensor killed direct latent decoding and broad action heads, while preserving sparse public-sensitive row-target support; local stress then shows the surviving action should be translated through a route-conserving S2 bridge rather than independent target moves.
surviving_mechanism: public-sensitive support -> row-target assignment -> route-conserving objective-stage bridge -> S2 listener/hub -> bounded submission
paper_safe_interpretation: HS-JEPA's paper contribution is the modular separation of hidden human-state orientation, target listener responsibility, row-target assignment, and route-conserving action decoding.  The leaderboard-specific part is the public sensor used to identify sparse support; the reusable mechanism is the decoder invariant and the boundary checks that reject collapsed or toxic latent actions.
